machinery_genes.quote: Eight orthologs of virB3, virB4, virB6, virB8, virB9, virB10, virB11, and virD4 were successfully amplified using genomic DNA extracted from A. tabida
machinery_genes.note: Rances et al 2008 functional characterization of the Wolbachia VirB/D4 T4SS. virB3 is part of cluster II ('The other cluster (herein designated cluster II) is composed of virB3, virB4, and virB6'). Apparatus paper used wAtab3/wRi but the operon structure is stated to be the same as in wMel (see notes); Pichon 2009 (PMID 19486895) shows the vir gene set is strictly conserved across 37 Wolbachia strains.

machinery_genes.quote: Eight orthologs of virB3, virB4, virB6, virB8, virB9, virB10, virB11, and virD4 were successfully amplified using genomic DNA extracted from A. tabida
machinery_genes.note: Cluster II component ('cluster II is composed of virB3, virB4, and virB6'). VirB4 IM ATPase role from the T4SS apparatus brief vocabulary.

machinery_genes.quote: Eight orthologs of virB3, virB4, virB6, virB8, virB9, virB10, virB11, and virD4 were successfully amplified using genomic DNA extracted from A. tabida
machinery_genes.note: Cluster II component, the only gene whose protein product was directly detected (anti-VirB6 antibodies). In wMel the virB6 region is annotated as putative membrane-spanning proteins WD0854 to WD0856 ('three are annotated as genes for putative membrane-spanning proteins (WD0854 to WD0856) in the wMel genome whereas the products of the corresponding coding sequences in the wBm genome are classified as VirB6 paralogs (Wbm0793 to Wbm0795)'); Wolbachia carries multiple virB6 paralogs at this locus. Locus tags noted for reference only; gene name is VirB6.

machinery_genes.quote: One cluster (herein designated cluster I) contains the genes virB8, virB9, virB10, virB11, and virD4.
machinery_genes.note: Rances et al 2008. Cluster I is the virB8-D4 operon, polycistronically transcribed and extending to wspB 'as in wMel'. VirB8 IM assembly-factor role from apparatus brief.

machinery_genes.quote: One cluster (herein designated cluster I) contains the genes virB8, virB9, virB10, virB11, and virD4.
machinery_genes.note: Cluster I (virB8-D4) component. VirB9 OM core-complex role from apparatus brief.

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane to outer-membrane channel ring (cluster I operon)
machinery_genes.quote: One cluster (herein designated cluster I) contains the genes virB8, virB9, virB10, virB11, and virD4.
machinery_genes.note: Cluster I (virB8-D4) component. VirB10 IM-OM channel-ring role from apparatus brief.

machinery_genes.quote: One cluster (herein designated cluster I) contains the genes virB8, virB9, virB10, virB11, and virD4.
machinery_genes.note: Cluster I (virB8-D4) component. VirB11 cytoplasmic ATPase role from apparatus brief.

machinery_genes.role: type-IV coupling protein (substrate-receptor ATPase; cluster I operon)
machinery_genes.quote: One cluster (herein designated cluster I) contains the genes virB8, virB9, virB10, virB11, and virD4.
machinery_genes.note: Cluster I (virB8-D4) component; the coupling protein / substrate receptor of the VirB/D4 apparatus. Distinct from translocated effectors, which are excluded.

notes: System: the Wolbachia VirB/D4 type-IVA secretion system. wMel is the obligate intracellular Wolbachia of Drosophila melanogaster (Wu et al 2004 genome, PMID 15024419). The Wolbachia T4SS is a REDUCED VirB/D4 set encoded at two separate genomic loci: cluster I / virB8-D4 operon (virB8, virB9, virB10, virB11, virD4, extending to the downstream wspB locus) and cluster II / virB3-B6 operon (virB3, virB4, virB6 plus virB6 paralogs and four downstream ORFs). All eight core machinery genes are named gene-by-gene in Rances et al 2008 (apparatus paper, PMID 18502862), which functionally characterized the system in strains wAtab3 and wRi and detected the VirB6 protein in wAlbB and wMelPop; the same operon architecture is stated to hold 'as in wMel' (cluster I extending to wspB, and the wMel virB6 locus annotated as WD0854-WD0856). Strain transfer is well supported: Pichon et al 2009 (PMID 19486895) showed vir gene sequence and organization are strictly conserved across 37 Wolbachia strains and that gene order within the virB8-D4 operon is conserved in all annotated Wolbachia genomes including wMel. DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED: virB1, virB2, virB5, virB7 are NOT part of the characterized Wolbachia VirB/D4 operons (the system is reduced and these canonical VirB genes were never named as Wolbachia components in the literature); the wspB locus (a surface-protein gene cotranscribed at the 3' end of cluster I, not a translocation-channel component); the four downstream ORFs of cluster II that are not annotated as vir genes; and the three curated effectors (E0495_02270/02275/06075) which are translocated substrates, not apparatus. No virB6 paralog was listed as a separate gene since the literature names them collectively as VirB6/virB6 paralogs rather than giving distinct apparatus-component names. Status COMPLETE: the full reduced Wolbachia VirB/D4 apparatus (8 genes across the two operons) is documented with verbatim naming quotes. (Note: one PMID I initially guessed for the Masui 2000 founding paper resolved to an unrelated article and was dropped; only verified PMIDs are listed.)
